hypotheses: Covid-19 humor will be perceived as more aversive compared to non-pandemic humor.
Individuals who use humor as a coping mechanism will find Covid-19 humor funnier and less disturbing.
claim.hypotheses_location: Summary of key findings in original_paper.pdf
claim.statement: The emotional response to Covid-19 humor, in terms of funniness and aversiveness, was compared against non-pandemic humor during Italy's nationwide lockdown.

data.source: Large-scale survey via social media and email
data.access_details: Approved by the University of Pavia's Ethics Committee
data.notes: Detailed demographic breakdown and psychological scales were used.

method.description: Survey assessing reactions to Covid-19 humor, incorporating personality and psychological distance factors.
steps: Conduct survey among 1,751 participants in Italy.
Measure responses to Covid-19 related humor versus non-pandemic humor.
Incorporate personality traits using Coping Humor Scale and Life Orientation Test.
Use psychological distance factors to assess impact on humor perception.
Utilize Linear Mixed Models for statistical analysis.
method.independent_variables: Humor type, Personality measures (CHS, LOTR), Psychological distance factors

results.summary: Covid-19 humor is generally more aversive but can be perceived as funnier by those using humor as a coping mechanism.

metadata.original_paper_title: Emotional response to Covid-19 humor during Italy's nationwide lockdown
